---
title: "Intro to AWS SageMaker for Predictive ML/AI"
author: 
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2025-11-07
date-format: long
image: "../../../images/SageMaker.png"

categories: 
  - Workshops
  - Code-along
  - Carpentries
  - Compute
  - AWS
  - GPU
  - Cloud
  - RAG
  - SageMaker
  - Bedrock

---

This introductory [AWS SageMaker workshop](https://carpentries-incubator.github.io/ML_with_AWS_SageMaker/) teaches core workflows for running predictive ML/AI models in AWS SageMaker, an AWS-managed machine learning environment. Participants will learn to set up data, configure SageMaker Notebooks, manage code repositories, train and tune models, and optimize resource costs effectively within AWS. Users will benefit from tips on controlling AWS expenses and scaling models efficiently, with real-world guidance on choosing appropriate CPU and GPU resources.

::: {.callout-tip}
## UW-Madison Cloud Users
A personal AWS account is fine for this workshop. However, for **long-term research use**, we recommend switching to a **UW-provisioned AWS account**. You'll get institutional pricing via [Internet2 NET+](https://internet2.edu/cloud/cloud-solutions-community/net-plus/), [lower overhead on grants](https://rsp.wisc.edu/proposalprep/cloudComputeInfo.cfm) (26% instead of 55.5% — saving ~$2,950 per $10k in cloud costs), data protection agreements (including BAA for HIPAA), and dedicated support from the [Public Cloud Team](https://kb.wisc.edu/page.php?id=109785). NIH-funded researchers can get additional discounts through the [STRIDES Initiative](https://kb.wisc.edu/109813).

**[Request a UW AWS account](https://kb.wisc.edu/sbsedirbs/page.php?id=104090)** | **[Why use a UW account?](https://kb.wisc.edu/page.php?id=109785)** | **[Full details: UW Cloud Services](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Compute/UW-Cloud-Services.html)**
:::

#### Cost estimate
Running through this workshop should cost approximately **$5-$10** on AWS, assuming moderate usage of GPU instances and a few parallel jobs (i.e., sticking to the lesson materials). For new AWS accounts, the **AWS Free Tier** may cover some of these costs, including 250 hours per month of the `ml.t2.medium` instance for the first two months, as well as some limited S3 storage. New users may be able to complete certain parts of the workshop for free or at a significantly reduced cost. We recommend monitoring usage through the AWS Billing Dashboard to stay within the free tier and manage any extra expenses effectively.

#### Prerequisites
- [**Workshop**: Intro to Machine Learning](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-ML_Sklearn.html)
- [**Workshop**: Basic Python Programming](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-Python_Gapminder.html)

#### Estimated time to complete 
**3-5 hours**: Based on running through training, tuning, and experimenting with example code setups.

## Related resources
- [**Compute**: UW-Madison Cloud Services (AWS, GCP, Azure)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Compute/UW-Cloud-Services.html) – Institutional discounts, lower grant overhead, data protections, research credits, and how to request a UW cloud account.
- [Public Cloud Team Office Hours](https://kb.wisc.edu/101516) – Drop-in hours on Thursdays, 2–3:15 PM via Zoom. Get answers to cloud-related questions from the RCI and Public Cloud Team.
- [AWS Free Tier Guide](https://aws.amazon.com/free): An overview of the AWS Free Tier, including limitations and expected costs for beginner users.
- [Introduction to AWS for Researchers (RCI)](https://researchci.it.wisc.edu/introduction-to-aws-for-researchers/) – UW-Madison RCI’s guide to getting started with AWS for research.
- [**Compute**: Intro to GCP for ML & AI](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-GCP.html) – Parallel workshop covering similar cloud ML concepts using GCP infrastructure.
- [**Compute**: BadgerCompute](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Compute/BadgerCompute.html) – UW–Madison’s lightweight, NetID-authenticated Jupyter service for short interactive sessions and classroom use. Includes a 4-hour runtime limit (which may sometimes beat the free version of Colab).
- [**Compute**: Google Colab](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Compute/GoogleColab.html) - Learn how to use Google Colab for machine learning workflows.
- [**Compute**: Center for High Throughput Computing (CHTC)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Compute/CHTC.html) - Learn how to use CHTC for machine learning jobs.
